After action(['UP','UP','LEFT']) there are three entries -- first UP, second UP, then LEFT. All change within one entry's frames is that single action animating, never several actions. Looking at the image, I can see:
- The white diamond is now on the left blue water region at [25, 25]
- The purple outline is now empty at [18, 57]
- The chain has reconfigured again
- The dark gray diamond at [49, 40] is now in the orange diamond outline position
- The purple square is at [33, 48]
- The orange circle is at [31, 49]
- The dark gray diamond at [41, 49] is still there
Wait, I notice something interesting. The dark gray diamond at [49, 40] is now in the orange diamond outline position. This is a new configuration I haven't seen before.
Let me think about what this means. The chain has reconfigured, and the dark gray diamond is now in the orange diamond outline. Maybe the goal is to get the white diamond into the orange diamond outline AND have the dark gray diamond in a specific position.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, I see that the dark gray diamond at [49, 40] is now in the orange diamond outline position. This suggests that the chain can move objects into the outlines.
Let me think about the pattern:
- When the white diamond is in the purple outline, the chain has a certain configuration
- When the white diamond is in the orange outline, the chain has a different configuration
- When the white diamond is on a dark gray diamond, the chain has yet another configuration
- When the white diamond is on the blue water, the chain has yet another configuration
Maybe the goal is to get the white diamond into the orange diamond outline AND have the dark gray diamond in the purple outline, or vice versa.
Let me try clicking on the orange diamond outline at [49, 40] to see if the white diamond moves there and if the level completes.
